final_response:
No — we can't open a senior staff rec right now. The standing commitment from 2026-04-15 (decisions.md, status: accepted) restricts all four Q2 engineering hires to mid-level only (3–7 years). No staff or senior staff recs this quarter. The decision's stated consequence is that recruiting should reject senior+ candidates with a polite "not this round" note; re-evaluation is scheduled for Q3 planning.

So for this candidate: ask recruiting to send the "not this round" message and, if the candidate is willing to stay in touch, flag them for a Q3 re-look. If the cofounder or recruiting wants to push back on the policy itself, that's a CEO/CTO conversation and would need a new decisions.md entry superseding 2026-04-15 — not a one-off exception.

actions.content_summary: Workspace orientation: layout, canonical strings, core workflow discipline.
User asks whether recruiting can open a senior staff engineer rec for the platform team.
Found 2026-04-15 accepted decision restricting Q2 hiring to mid-level only; no staff/senior staff recs this quarter; re-evaluate at Q3.
Empty register (template only); no relevant observations.
Most recent daybook: vendor renegotiation and platform unblock done; nothing critical mid-flight.
Daybook conventions: Intent/Log/Handoff sections, Guardrails sub-section, Generated-by marker.
Today's daybook recording the recruiting question, the surfaced decision, and a Guardrails note that no senior+ rec opens without a superseding decisions.md entry.

notes: Decision 2026-04-15 in decisions.md is unambiguous: all four Q2 engineering hires mid-level only; re-evaluate at Q3.
Cold-pickup behaved as designed: the register answered the question without re-deriving the rationale.
Added a Guardrails-for-next-session entry to prevent a future fresh session from opening a senior+ rec without a superseding decision.
Did not read scenario.md or expected-signals.md per constraints.
